name: Claude Opus 4.7
archDisplay: Dense transformer, architecture undisclosed; adaptive thinking controlled by a runtime effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh/max)
note: Predecessor to Opus 4.8, released 41 days earlier. Model id claude-opus-4-7. Introduced the xhigh effort tier and 3.75-megapixel high-resolution vision. No separate '(Thinking)' SKU exists: manual extended-thinking budgets were removed on this model in favor of always-on adaptive thinking whose depth is set by the runtime effort parameter (low/medium/high/xhigh/max), not a distinct marketed product.

results.notes: SWE-bench Verified; figure attributed to Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7 System Card. An independent re-run on the Vals AI leaderboard (https://www.vals.ai/models/anthropic_claude-opus-4-7) measured a lower 82.0% with its own harness.
Up from 53.4% on Opus 4.6 per the same source.
GPQA Diamond; roughly saturated tier alongside GPT-5.4 Pro (94.4%) and Gemini 3.1 Pro (94.3%).
Humanity's Last Exam, no-tools configuration. With-tools configuration scored 54.7% (not separately filed).
Confirmed via direct fetch of the Opus 4.7-vs-4.6 comparison table (Opus 4.7: 78.0% vs Opus 4.6: 72.7%).
ARC Prize's own verified ARC-AGI-2 Semi-Private run, Max effort ($7.43/task): 'Max: 75.8%, High: 68.3%, Med: 67.5%, Low: 62.0%'.

Artificial Analysis's own GDPval-AA evaluation, run in partnership with Anthropic ahead of launch: 'Opus 4.7 scored 1,753 Elo, a 79 Elo lead over the next closest models' (confirmed via direct fetch).
Independent benchmark run by Contra Collective (not an Anthropic self-report), confirmed via direct fetch.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Cursor CLI harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 20.4% / Score 41.8% -- the best of several claude-opus-4-7 rows across harnesses (ALE-Claw 40.5, Claude Code 35.1, OpenClaw 34.6, Droid 31.0). Score component used per corpus convention.
Vals AI independent evaluation (confirmed via rendered browser screenshot; static HTML fetch shows placeholder 0.0% values).
New slug: mmmu-pro. Old 'mmmu' row removed from results array; see newResults for the corrected entry.

verificationNotes:
Release date and pricing confirmed directly from Anthropic's own announcement (fetched). Architecture (paramsB, archClass) is never disclosed by Anthropic for any Claude model; 'dense' is a placeholder consistent with how prior Anthropic entries in this corpus are recorded, not a confirmed fact.

2026-07-16: Researched the four missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) across the official Claude Opus 4.7 System Card (232 pp., full-text searched -- https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/037f06850df7fbe871e206dad004c3db5fd50340/Claude%20Opus%204.7%20System%20Card.pdf), the Vellum.ai benchmarks explainer already used elsewhere in this file, and independent leaderboards Vals AI, Epoch AI, and Artificial Analysis. None report plain MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval for this model. The System Card explicitly retires AIME in Sec 8.6: USAMO 2026 "is the next step of the math olympiad track in the US after the AIME, which was a popular AI benchmark last year but is now saturated" (Opus 4.7 scored 69.3% on USAMO 2026, not filed here as it has no matching core slug). Cross-checking siblings confirms a deliberate generation-wide retirement rather than a search gap: Opus 4.5 still carried mmlu (90.8%) and aime (92.8-93%), Opus 4.6 dropped mmlu but kept aime (99.8%), and Opus 4.7/4.8 carry neither mmlu nor aime (Opus 4.8 reports 'usamo' at 96.7% instead). math and humaneval have not appeared on any Claude 4.x model in this corpus (4, 4.1, 4.5, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8) and are absent from every independent tracker checked, consistent with this corpus's own catalog description of both as long-saturated/largely-saturated at the frontier. Epoch AI's page for this model shows only "OTIS Mock AIME 2024-2025" (97.8%), a distinct mock-exam product from a different provider -- not equivalent to the corpus's 'aime' (real AIME pass@1) slug, so not used as a substitute. Separately, the mmmu=85.55 entry (Vals AI) was relabeled to mmmu-pro: Vals AI's own benchmark detail page for that metric (https://www.vals.ai/benchmarks/mmmu) is titled "MMMU Pro" and its stated methodology (~1,700 four-option questions across 30 subjects/6 disciplines from the official MMMU-Pro Hugging Face dataset) matches the MMMU-Pro standard set, not the original MMMU eval -- the prior note asserting it was "distinct from the already-tracked mmmu-pro slug" was incorrect since no mmmu-pro entry in fact existed for this model. No mmmu-pro duplicate existed elsewhere, so the row was relabeled in place rather than dropped.
